summary: Typosquat of axios under @qwedqwed scope pulling unrelated `math` transitive dependency
details: Package `@qwedqwed/axios` copies the legitimate axios codebase, README, and metadata (author Matt Zabriskie, axios homepage and git repo) verbatim but is published under the unrelated `@qwedqwed` scope. The published version 1.16.6 does not exist upstream and the bundled VERSION constant reads 1.16.1, indicating a repackage rather than a fork. The dependency list in package.json (lines 39-44) adds `"math": "^0.0.3"` — a package the real axios does not depend on. A developer who runs `npm install @qwedqwed/axios` thinking they are installing axios receives the genuine axios runtime plus an attacker-selected transitive dependency that has no functional role in axios. The lure package itself contains no lifecycle scripts and no malicious code in dist/node/axios.cjs; the harm is delivered via the silently added `math` transitive being resolved into the installer's dependency tree.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
